AgroCouncil Presents · May 2026

“Greenport in Bloom”

A Month-Long Celebration of Cherry Blossom Season

Five Weekends · Five Experiences

With more than 300 cherry blossom trees representing seven distinct varieties, Greenport's blooms unfold in stages — ensuring visitors can experience blossoms somewhere along the Petal Tour throughout the entire month of May.

Festival Highlights

Five Weekends of Events

Opening Weekend
Saturday, May 2
5K Race & Festival Launch

Run for the Petals 5K at 9:00 AM led by Nicki Gohorel. Present your race bib for opening day brunch at American Beech. Businesses unveil blossom-themed displays across the village.

Second Weekend
May 8–9 · Mother's Day
Guided Petal Tour

First-ever guided walking Petal Tour with Tree Committee member John Quinlan. Select trees adorned with pink ribbons. Special Mother's Day promotions from participating businesses.

Third Weekend
Friday, May 15
Stories in the Sky: Kite Adventure

2:30–4:00 PM at Floyd Memorial Library. Grades 1–12 explore Hanami, write Sakura no kotoba, decorate kites, and learn to fly them in the library garden. Free admission.

Fourth Weekend
Memorial Day Weekend
Vendor Village & Experiences

Festival vendor offerings spotlight local makers, food, and experiences. Drink specials, cherry blossom themed gifts, treats and more at participating businesses.

Final Weekend
Saturday, May 30 · 2:30 PM
Taiko Closing Celebration

Japanese Taiko drummers by CAST & Ryu Shu Kan Japanese Art Center. Interactive demonstration at Holy Trinity Episcopal Church, Main Street, Greenport.

Support the Festival

Become a Sponsor

Help us grow Greenport in Bloom! Sponsorships support the Greenport Village Tree Committee, the BID Beautification Committee, and community programming throughout the year.

2026 Petal Tour

Interactive Map

This year, the Petal Tour map is available exclusively in digital format — explore the village at your own pace and discover blossoms, events, and seasonal offerings throughout May.
